summary: Children with germline retinoblastoma inherit a defective RB1 gene, increasing their lifetime risk of secondary cancers due to genomic instability. Osteosarcoma is the most commonly reported secondary malignancy in this group, supported by extensive evidence including longitudinal studies and surveillance reports.
